mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of ML models while optimizing performance through intelligent routing and caching. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient hardware resources and the absence of dependencies on other systems like Istio or Knative.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official KubeAI Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeAI Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_inference_crd.yaml

```

> Install the KubeAI Model CRD.
> Install the KubeAI Inference CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs KubeAI into the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeAI pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

kubectl get crd

```

> List all pods in the kubeai namespace to verify the installation.
> Check if the KubeAI CRDs are installed correctly.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all models and inference resources managed by KubeAI, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstalls the KubeAI release.
> Deletes the kubeai namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to back up your models and inference resources. This step will upgrade KubeAI to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-models-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Backup existing models.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the KubeAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-models-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may already exist or there may be a network issue.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and delete them if necessary, then retry the installation.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' to diagnose the issue and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that KubeAI has not been installed correctly or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure KubeAI is installed in the correct namespace with 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Check RBAC settings and ensure the service account has the required roles and permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
